# penrose_tiling

**Theme**: "Quasicrystal" — Penrose P3 aperiodic rhombus tiling; fat (72°) and thin (36°) rhombuses generated by 7 rounds of Robinson-triangle substitution from a decagonal seed; the pattern fills the plane without ever repeating, yet maintains perfect 5-fold rotational symmetry.

**Technique**: Binary triangle subdivision (`type 0` fat / `type 1` thin), 7 iterations from a 10-triangle decagonal wheel; 5-sector angular coloring (centroid angle ÷ 72°) reveals the quasiperiodic structure with warm tones for fat rhombuses and cool tones for thin.

**Description**: Starting from 10 fat triangles arranged as a decagonal medallion, each iteration applies the golden-ratio substitution rule (P → A + (B−A)/φ) to split triangles into smaller ones. After 7 generations, ~6100 triangles form ~3000 interlocked rhombuses. Fat halves are colored in 5 warm shades (gold, coral, amber) by angular sector; thin halves use 5 cool shades (cerulean, teal, periwinkle). Dark edges give each tile crisp definition.

**Palette**:
- Background: `#05080f` deep navy-black
- Fat rhombuses: warm gold · coral · amber · yellow-gold · light amber (5-sector)
- Thin rhombuses: cerulean · teal · violet-blue · green-teal · periwinkle (5-sector)

**Preview**: `preview.png`
